NL: This policy grants global read access to the patients table to all medical staff. In a hospital environment, it is crucial that any physician can consult a patient’s basic file (identification, contact) to initiate care, regardless of whether they are the assigned physician or not. This base rule has no additional constraints.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY shysician_reads_all_patients ON Patients
FOR SELECT
TO physician
USING (true);

NL: System administrators have full control over employee records. This policy allows them to create, modify, and delete records in the Employees table. It is a “superuser” policy for human resources management within the technical system.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY admin_full_access_empleados ON Empleados
FOR ALL
TO admin
USING (true)
WITH CHECK (true);

NL: This policy defines the audit profile. Auditors must have unrestricted read access to medical records and billing to perform their control tasks, but are explicitly prohibited from any modification actions. This separation of duties ensures data integrity during audits.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY auditor_read_only_historial ON Historial_Clinico
FOR SELECT
TO auditor
USING (true);

CREATE POLICY auditor_read_only_facturacion ON Facturacion
FOR SELECT
TO auditor
USING (true);

CREATE POLICY auditor_no_write ON Historial_Clinico
FOR UPDATE, DELETE, INSERT
TO auditor
USING (false);

CREATE POLICY auditor_no_write_fact ON Facturacion
FOR UPDATE, DELETE, INSERT
TO auditor
USING (false);

NL: This policy ensures that each patient has exclusive read access to their own medical record. It is based on an identity constraint: the ID of the user requesting access (Assignee) must match the patient ID associated with the resource (Target). This protects the privacy of medical data, ensuring that a patient cannot see others’ records.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY paciente_own_records ON Historial_Clinico
FOR SELECT
TO paciente
USING (patient_id = current_user_id());

NL: Physicians can only modify or add notes to the medical records of patients they are assigned to. This policy does not limit reading (covered by P01), but writing. It prevents physicians not involved in a patient’s treatment from altering their record, maintaining responsibility and traceability of changes.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY medico_write_own_assigned ON Historial_Clinico
FOR UPDATE
TO medico
USING (assigned_doctor_id = current_user_id())
WITH CHECK (assigned_doctor_id = current_user_id());

CREATE POLICY medico_insert_own_assigned ON Historial_Clinico
FOR INSERT
TO medico
WITH CHECK (assigned_doctor_id = current_user_id());

NL: The head of a medical department needs to supervise all patients admitted to their area, regardless of which individual physician is treating them. This policy grants read access based on the match of the “Department” attribute between the head (user) and the patient (resource).

In life-or-death situations, privacy yields to immediate medical necessity.This policy allows emergency physicians to access records of patients marked with status “CRITICAL” or “EMERGENCY” even if they are not their assigned patients. This is a “break-the-glass” mechanism conditioned by the patient’s status.

Researchers can access clinical data for statistical and scientific analysis, but only if said data has been previously anonymized. This policy protects patient identity while allowing secondary use of data under strict privacy conditions.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY investigador_anonymized_only ON Historial_Clinico
FOR SELECT
TO investigador
USING (is_anonymized = true);

NL: This is a financial business policy. Administrative staff are explicitly prohibited from scheduling new appointments for patients who have outstanding debts to the hospital. The system checks the financial status (DEBTOR) before allowing the insertion of a new appointment.

Nurses need access to patients’ medication to administer it, but only during their working hours. This temporal policy restricts access (reading and recording administration) exclusively to the time interval defined by the nurse’s shift, improving security and reducing the risk of access outside regular hours.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY enfermero_shift_access ON Medicacion
FOR SELECT, UPDATE
TO enfermero
USING (
CURRENT_TIME >= (
SELECT shift_start FROM Empleados
WHERE id = current_user_id()
)
AND
CURRENT_TIME <= (
SELECT shift_end FROM Empleados
WHERE id = current_user_id()
)
);

NL: This policy authorizes physicians to create and modify records in the Medication table (prescribe drugs). It is an operational capability policy: only qualified personnel (physicians) should have write permission in this critical table. Nurses (P10) only read or update administration, and pharmacists (P12) only dispense.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY medico_prescribe_meds ON Medicacion
FOR INSERT, UPDATE
TO medico
WITH CHECK (true); -- Assigned patient restriction applies in P05

NL: The hospital pharmacist is responsible for dispensing prescribed medication.They have read access to view prescriptions and limited update permission to change the medication status from “PENDING” to “DISPENSED.” They cannot alter the dosage or the drug prescribed.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY pharma_dispense ON Medicacion
FOR SELECT, UPDATE
TO farmaceutico
USING (status = 'PENDIENTE')
WITH CHECK (status = 'DISPENSADO');

NL: Sometimes it is necessary to refer a patient to an external specialist for a second opinion. This policy grants temporary access to the patient’s record to the external physician, conditioned on the existence of an active and valid referral.

Legal guardians or parents have the right to access the medical history of patients under their care, provided they are minors. Once the patient reaches 18 years of age, this policy automatically ceases to apply to protect the adult’s privacy.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY guardian_read_minor ON Historial_Clinico
FOR SELECT
TO familiar
40
USING (
patient_id IN (
SELECT id FROM Pacientes
WHERE guardian_id = current_user_id()
AND age < 18
)
);

NL: Laboratory technicians generate data (analysis results) that must be stored in the system. They have permission to create new result records, but for privacy and data minimization reasons, they do not have permission to read the patient’s full clinical history—only the results they generate themselves or the test orders.
RLS:
CREATE POLICY lab_insert_results ON Resultados_Pruebas
FOR INSERT
TO tecnico_lab
WITH CHECK (true);
-- Policy to allow reading only their own inserted
-- results or open orders can be added separately
